pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn map of the Ardèche river route. At the top, a volcanic lake labelled 'Le lac d'Issarlès' with a sketch of a mountain peak labelled 'Gerbier de Jonc'. To the upper right, 'Col de Vieux [?Dombier]' with notes about washing clothes in river and a swim pool at campsite. A sinuous river line labelled 'R. Ardèche' runs from Aubenas (top, with a dot) southward. Joyeuse is marked to the left mid-river. Chassezac joins from the left near Chaubon(as), annotated '5 hour canoes'. Lower right shows Chames with '4 hour canoes', then caves, and at the bottom Gr St. Martial and St. Martial d'Ardèche. Arrow pointing north at top. Campsite/château sketch near Chaubon(as).
pages.notes: Page is a hand-drawn annotated route sketch rather than narrative prose. 'Gerbier de Jonc' has a word before it that could read 'mid' or '[?find]' or similar — unclear. 'Col de Vieux' followed by a word that could be 'Dombier' or 'Lombier'. 'Chaubon' could also be 'Chaubonas' or 'Chaubon as' (two words). The text '[?Pont] l'Arc' may be 'Pont d'Arc' — well-known Ardèche landmark. No year is legible on this page; context (Ardèche canoe trip, family names Simon/Eve/Peter/Thomas/William) matches other entries in the volume.

pages.full_text:
up the rapids to try again. Eve was exceedingly proud of her non-capsizing record almost to the end of the trip, when she + Peter tipped over + were rescued by a naked man from the Naturist beaches. Peter was very brave, quite fearless about the next rapids. Eve lost a shoe, but found someone else's straight away.
   By 4 o'clock Simon found that he had misread the map + we were only ½ way back to base, not ¾, as he had thought. Fortunately we (Simon) had brought far more food + drink than we needed. Thomas was very tired. William got blisters. At times there was a bad head wind.
   When we got triumphantly home we went out for steak + chips; it was a children's restaurant, so they played games + we had icecreams while Simon went home with[...]
pages.notes: Text continues from a previous page (page 52, not visible in this spread) and is cut off at the bottom of the page. Mentions family members Eve, Peter, Simon, Thomas, William. The passage describes the end of a canoe trip on the Ardèche. Page number 53 is visible top-right. Some text in the left gutter margin appears to be bleed-through from the left page route sketch labels (e.g. 'St. Martial d'Ardèche'). No year is explicitly stated on this page.
